channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Sergey says: "Just use Chainlink CCIP." — 46 replies — 184.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sergey says: "Just use Chainlink CCIP."

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-06-23 22:57 UTC

OP

Sergey says: "Just use Chainlink CCIP."

Early replies (first 3)

62388705
>>62388673
Loving his use of hamburger grease in his hair.

62388723
I have been seeing this creature for years now but I never really stopped to take a closer look. I regret doing that today as every feature of this unfortunate man is completly appalling.

62388769
>>62388723

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62393492
>>62388673

62393595
Im buying 7500 GLNK in my IRA tomorrow. Try and talk me out of it

62393623
>jeetlink thread on the first page

62393645
>>62393595
worst case is that you lose your money while everyone else is making steady gains on stocks, so i say go for it. it's your money

62393653
>>62393645
Everything else i look at is so pumped already. Everyone's portfolio i look at is full of AI shit. I already have exposure to all that noise. Chainlink and Gamestop are my only holdings that aren't pumped up like crazy right now. Chainlink in particular is due for a good rebound

62393746
>>62393595
I've been loading my HSA with glnk, so yeah go for it anon.

62393862
>>62393653
>Due for a pump
Why? Everyone that gets involved with it that isn't Sergey gets covered in shit. Look at what happened to the former CTO of SWIFT. Children usually learn to not touch a hot stove after burning themselves twice, even the mentally retarded learn this around the 9th or 10th time. Meanwhile, linkies have been getting burned for 5 straight years and still haven't learned a thing lmao.

62393925
>>62393862
What happened to the CTO?

62394776
>>62390366
>They are collapsing public services and they're going to privatise everything from the police to the army, education and NASA. The running down of all of these institutions is deliberate and designed to force people to look for privately owned solutions.
what is the point of paying taxes then if they're not going to do anything useful for us.

62394797
>>62393595
Why not wait for the capitulation event at this point?

62394807
>>62394776
You must be 18 years old to post here buddy. They’ll throw you in prison if you aren’t funding Jewish wars and genocide. No social cohesion due to (((diversity))), bread and circus (sports ball and gambling) stops the goy cattle from organizing and revolting. Grim desu

62394831
price eod July 4th?!

62394850
>>62393925
you're supposed to think it's bad for some reason when a guy stops working someplace and goes someplace else
>What happened to the CTO?
wish the question would get answered, but I doubt it
some ai chainlink stuff, wonder if kemal is involved I dunno

62394998
>>62394850
>Guys just step down from working at a world renowned financial engine to a no name AI bullshit startup after their social media feeds get filled with retarded frog posters asking for $100 shitcoins all the time
Lmao.

62395151
>>62394998
>muh prestige
>muh x retards
I'm not buying it

62395224
>>62388723
Way sexier then you

62395987
>>62388673
Why would someone pay 100x rent seeking fee to use a project that is 100x slower than competitors? CCIP is COMPLETELY uncompetitive which is why Tom Schach was fired for failing to foresee and plan for literally the only thing he has had to to prevent his company from losing the literally biggest moat since Blockbuster.

62396593
>>62395987
>capitalizing WORDS for EMPHASIS don't cha know!?
>moar larp
>fresh ID
you fucking faggots

62398003
>>62394776
You're already getting less and less while paying more and more and nobody seems to care enough to do anything about it.
Welfare is being used as wealth extraction for the middle and working classes and funding rent seeking and corporate profits.
Look at the absolute state of most Western cities?

62398092
>>62394831
$6.69

This is financial advice

62398281
>>62394776
NPC wagies don’t bother to think

62398326
>>62393653
best to stay with what you know. why risk experiencing gains when you can get your safe -2% per month? good call

62398497
>>62398304
https://x.com/MSBIntel/status/2069123251184218230
Chainlink flipped Cardano

62398501
>>62395987
read the CCIP v2 changelog
this ccip transaction was 19 seconds

62399128
>>62388673

meanwhile

>>62399084

62403154
>>62388673
Sergey says: Money shouldn't have business hours

62403195
>>62403154
It shouldn’t go into the toilet either

62403216
>>62403154
Why? So it’s easier to use it to traffic people? Go fuck yourself fat chainlink pedo

62403225
>>62403216
>>62403195
welcome to the chainlink thread

item.title: What made you still hold BTC? — 104 replies — 150.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
What made you still hold BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 104 • Updated: 2026-06-23 22:28 UTC

OP

Bitcoin has gone through multiple cycles, crashes, and endless criticism, yet many continue to hold. What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

Early replies (first 3)

62371733
>>62371718
>What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

62371807
The only reason to ever sell bitcoin is to buy more at a lower price.

62371917
>>62371807
correct
>waiting for a good entry

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62387566
Im fine

62387833
>>62387563
Depends what you mean by valuable. A lot of the hype is gone, but people are still borrowing, lending, and farming yield.

62387836
>>62387549
Doge coins lol

62387875
>>62371718
Because it's the only crypto asset I'd be comfortable holding for the next decade without constantly checking the chart.

62387892
the longer this market exists, the more Bitcoin seems to become the benchmark everything else gets compared against. So very very bullish

62388642
>>62387875
100%

62388704
>>62371718
Institutions are starting to make it into BTC, before bridge was the issue but with TBV eliminating that, more money will start flowing into BTC, it still feels early.

62388780
>>62376610
> printing is the only thing keeping the bubble from popping instantly. you want that?

62388819
>>62377444
> nothing has changed except the price chart and your cope levels

62388854
nothing, im not retarded so i take profits. my cost basis is negative now.

62389149
>>62388780
You know my answer for that

62389340
I feel like something new has to happen before the market shifts sentiment.

All the ETFs and government regulation just hurt the project.
Nobody is utilizing the tools, the tools still feel beta, and developers are being arrested.

If it's just going to be a big gay digital rock the momentum grinds to a halt.

The problem is some of us got rich and have something to lose now.
Different factions formed inside the developer community.
Is there incentive to free ball the network still? idk.

I'm still holding but this bear feels different

62390164
>>62388819
So how will you change it?

62391118
>>62389340
I'm still holding as well.

62391156
>>62371718
It feels like shit when it goes down but when you're near the bottom like now you just remember the last times you felt this way and how it gongo up from here, everytime. Bobos are ngmi. Mumu supremacy is real. Cope trannies.

62391165
>>62383619
Based retard

62391192
>>62371718
The 20% long-term-capital-gains tax rate that I'd have to pay if I cashed out all at once.

62391471
The only investments I hold are schizo nonsense Chainlink and PMs. I have a good amount of money through my Roth and 401k. I can only invest in specific etfs through my work 401k. What’s the best method to get long term Bitcoin exposure through a IRA?

62392524
>>62391156
You're not a real investor

62393205
>>62372380
Then how come I can send BTC to my Bitcoin core wallet dumbfuck?

62393451
>>62371718
I believe in the philosophy that led to bitcoin. Sadly the vast majority of humanity are just cattle. I still hold because I believe there is something that can happen. The pessimism on this board doesn't change my view on this. I will hold and if it fails I'll be ok because I have stocks and silver and land.

62395366
>>62391192
That's too much to be honest

62395566
>>62371718
I plan on selling when I retire in ten years. But who knows maybe I will change my mind and keep holding.

62396187
All these retards are greedy, that's all there is to it

62398052
>>62395566
Keep going anon

62399672
>>62396187
Oh really huh?

62399688
>>62371718
I forgot the passwords for one account and everything got stolen from the other.

62401169
>>62399688
Story maker

62403086
>>62393205
I guess you know the answer

62403127
>>62371718
If you all feel fucked, just remember some farmer in Australia sold all his life saving and put $10 million onto s&p6900.
I bet that cunt has necked himself now.

item.title: VOO and Chill — 32 replies — 124.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-06-23 22:53 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62400564
>>62400560
>RICH
lol good one anon
you may be ok, but you won't be rich

62400620
>>62400564
What makes one rich then, can only doing a good trade here and there achieve this
And depending on the definition, being “OK” probably puts you in the 85th percentile versus people in credit card debt at least
I’ve considered trying to be more sector specific or something but idk

62400633
>>62400620
when you are passively producing about 200k in cash annually. that allows you unrestricted living in any part of the US. you need at least 4 mil+ folio to do that comfortably.

62400636
Thing is, index funds are for either:
A) People who have a lot of money where % changes magnify in a meaningful way
B) People who start early enough in life and consistently enough for compounding to mean anything

The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.

>b-but something is better than nothing!
Yes and I'm sure that used car and maybe a holiday you can buy with it when you're 65 from cashing out all your investments will be great.....

62400638
>>62400633
You can that with VOO and chill though…

62400645
>>62400638
>5% withdrawal rate

YIKES
DESU you need 5mil, and you aren't getting there without constantly invest invest invest and wa

62400651
>>62400645
*waiting and hoping you don't die along the way.

62400652
>>62400554
ngmi

62400699
>>62400652
Exactly you can’t explain it
>>62400636
Well I’m 26 and have low expenses so I’ve been DCA my salary into VOO
I guess at 26 I could have more risk but idk this is hitting 20% returns after a year and a half good enough for me

62400995
Qqq is just more narrow and carries more risk. It’s true that s&p and nasdaq carry many of the same companies right now, but nasdaq can crash and fail to recover, or growth can move to another segment of the economy. S&P will adjust when either of these happen. Nasdaq will be Nasdaq. It seems if you invest in both that’s fine, but you are putting your money in the same place twice and increasing risk.

62401051
>>62400995
From what I’ve seen QQQ has basically just mirrored VOO with a factor of 2
Twice the gains, twice the loses and actually quite a few years where the two were equal
I think a decade long CAGR comparison was a 5% difference but I’m not sure if CAGR is the best stat to go by?

62401312
What a pointless thread. Oh wow have you come to the conclusion you should just buy an ETF wow what a waste of space this shit thread is.

62401466
>>62401312
Sorry I should’ve made a thread where I pretend to buy a stock and ask what I’m in for instead
Or posting a red angry basedjak

62401489
>>62401312
ngmi

62401790
>>62400620
Putting in 100 bucks per month makes you average.

62402005
>>62400493
VWCE and chill

62402931
>>62401790
Any image with a wojak means you should follow the opposite of whatever advice it is advocating
Additionally
>100 bucks a month
LMAO ARE YOU POOR

62402981
ETFs only make sense if you're already rich and looking for a source of passive income

62402983
>>62402981
no they don't lmao

62402988
>>62402981
>going for that passive income

62402991
>>62402983
Yeah he's right if you have enough capital you can easily live off it. The problem is morons with 10k thinking VOO is going to make them rich after investing for a few years.

62402995
>>62402991
no he isn't idiot

62403030
>>62402931
The average person is pretty poor. And this is just braindead fire and forget index fund investing. 100 or 120 per month gives you 200k in 40 years.
You can put in 500 or 600 per month if you want a million in 40 years. Of course it won't be a million, it will be more like 5 million or 10 million due to hyper inflation, but it will be equal to what a million is today.

62403034
>>62402981
ETFs are how you make generational wealth without thinking. It literally requires no work. Picking stocks burns brainpower and also has a high failure rate.

62403044
>>62402991
lmao no, etfs are good for retirement as a middle class wagecuck; they wont make you rich sure but to claim is only useful for millionaires is bullshit.
Also getting rich quick is literally the point of all this.

62403053
>>62403044
The median 64 year old american has 400k to retire on and they seem to be ok with it.
That's what middle class means in america. Of course 1 in 10 Americans have a net worth of 1 million, but that's not controlled for age.

62403140
>>62400636
Im the second one.
Yeehaw

62403184
>>62403140
same

62403215
>>62403030
>The average person is pretty poor.

Kind of.

>The U.S. median household income is $83,730. For individual personal income, the median figure is $45,140. -- St. Louis FED

Most Americans can afford to save more than $100/month, especially if they are married or have a SO, but most Americans really don't think about the future, so they finance the best lifestyle the bank is willing to finance instead of living well beneath their means. Most Americans should not be going to Disneyworld or on vacations every year, especially on credit, but they do and this leads them to get into debt, making it "impossible" to save money.

I try to save as much as possible because no way am I not going to settle for $200k in the year of our Lord 2060. That's broke money today, much less in 40 years when the USD is further in the toilet.

item.title: Irl crime — 60 replies — 75.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Irl crime

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-06-23 22:21 UTC

OP

Share crime stories and currently relevant opportunities ITT. IRL shit, not online scamming.

Inb4
>muh bankers muh politicians
Wow, very funny, what an original joke

Early replies (first 3)

62393319
>>62393307
I fucked your mom.

62393322
12 years ago my first job was in a supermarket. I randomly decided to steal a candy bar for no reason, but i felt so guilty that I confessed to my supervisor a day later and offered to pay. She said it was okay, and nothing happened.

62393326
>>62393319
This guy paid me $50 so he could fuck your mom.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62397020
>>62396900
I'm a low civilization tier subhuman. I don't give a fuck about your gay "civilization.

62397200
>>62393307
When I was a young teenager, I had a friend who bought a bb gun pistol. He then waited for evening, went to the park, hid behind the bushes near a path, and then jumped out at old people walking and stole their wallets and jewelry. He made a lot of money. He asked me to join in, but I wasn't interested.

No idea what happened to him. Probably dead. Good riddance.

62397518
Bump

62397557
>>62394022
What a literal fucking nigger. Repent

62397565
>>62396900
Retards posting obviously fake quotes is always funny

62397959
>>62397557
Kys

62398384
>>62393307
Me on the picrel

62398550
>>62393307
>>62393356
>>62394022
>>62395812
>>62397020
>>62397565
>>62397959
Izzat farmers
Abandon thread

62398943
Bump

62399147
>>62393307
Sleep Tight Pigger

62399469
This one time my bros and i knew about 35 mil worth of gold bars so we painted the floor beneath their safe with explosives and then had a decoy boat drive off as we cracked the safe in the water below. Then we scuba'd the bars to a seperate boat, and wbile celebrating one of our own bros decided to double cross us as we celebrated our heist. Fuckin loser didnt even know what to do with all that money besides copy all our ideas. So anyway we come up with a plan to steal the gold back by sending our dead safe cracker's daughter (whom the coward was unfamiliar with) to sneak into his house disguised as a television internet repair woman, and we cut the lines of his tv. Our plans were almost ruined when his neighbors had a house party so we called it off and had to rethink our plans; we surveilled him and found out he was moving our gold in 3 trucks so we modded 3 mini coopers all souped up and pulled another demolished floor trick, the loser viewing from the helicopter couldnt see because we had a billboard truck that was blocking the view of the gold truck falling into the subway below. We knew which truck the gold was in because the ride height and our hackerbro friend basically led them right into a traffic jam above the explosive floor. After incapacitating the drivers we cracked the safe and transported the gold in our minis to a train. Our former betrayor tried to buy his way onto the train only to find that we paid off the mobsters that owned the train a ton of cash and gold to be part of our plan too. Then we listened to money by pink floyd.

62399612
>>62399469

That hackerbro's name? Albert Einstein.

62399967
>>62393322
I one time worked at Walmart a summer in high school and someone was cutting up bags to eat handfuls and I had to clean it. They didn’t give me a lunch break because I was so new and I was throwing away dozens of bags so I ate a single chip in a bag that was popped but not fully opened. Got fired on the spot as they were watching me on video like a hawk. Fuck that gay company. I’ve never bought from them since

62400055
>>62399967
Lol did you learn your retard-goy lesson?

62400082
>>62393319
Bestiality is technically illegal

62400093
I have not and will commit any crime as I am a model citizen and do not break the law.
Anything I have posted on this site that contradicts that is fairy tales and only a fool would take those such posts as fact.

62400095
>>62400093
Will NOT commit*
God fucking damn

62400096
>>62400093
Wow that's really clever and original and funny

62400920
>>62393307
comfy

62400948
>>62393307
You call that a crime scene?
Here’s a real big one bed in the street you lightweight!

62401887
Bump

62401956
This is the most pathetic fed agent trap Ive ever seen.
You guys, speaking to LE here, realize that most people on 4chan are just regular people? Maybe a little edgier and weirder? Go fuck yourself.

62401967
>>62393307
I stole some dinosaur toys from my younger cousin. My aunt and uncle obviously noticed but let it slip because they knew I was a poor kid.

Also, I guess picking up banknotes after old people drop them once they get up from public transportation seating? I have "stolen" like 20 bucks in my life that way. Why do the old and disabled retards don't give a shit about losing money, anyways?

62401994
>>62401956
>Listen here kid, you HAVE to follow arbitrary Jewish law!

62401997
I worked at my Mom's job for a Boomercare home when Covid first broke out. There was a massive shortage of nurses so a good amount of the tasks were definitely illegal since I was just some guy in zoom college and didn't have any qualifications to be anywhere near any of the patients, but it was very comfy and ironically helped me get the job I work now more than that college diploma did lol

62402026
>>62393307
>Dusty summer sky
>Eucalyptus
>Wild boar
>yellow plates
>Commie block
>Paper only recycling bins
Israel, My guess is within 250meters of downtown tel aviv
Watch yourself Lior, you are glowing.

62402123
>>62393424
The only thing he was right about tho was buying bitcoin early. You don’t need to do crime for that.

62402179
>>62401956
Yeah I'm an NSA agent trying to catch criminals using this thread.

>>62402026
Maybe the Hebrew writing would've been the clue to follow here, Detective Dipshit.
On no, looks like you've uncovered my glowie operation for Tel Aviv!


Jews are absolutely right about goyim and niggers are right about hickeys and jeets are tight about timmies.
There's a very good reason for your current predicament

62402182
>>62402179
Honkeys*
Whitoids

62403110
>62402179
Fag

item.title: Crypto is fucked because you're buying tokens that were minted out of thin air. — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Crypto is fucked because you're buying tokens that were minted out of thin air.

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-23 22:59 UTC

OP

Crypto is fucked because you're buying tokens that were minted out of thin air. OG crypto, biggest example being bitcoin, started out being mineable only. Now niggas buy some bullshit with a 100M+ valuation right when it hits the market, even though the cost to produce was 0, and wonder why they get dumped on eternally even as the price gets way oversold. News flash dipshit: those tokens were free for someone

Early replies (first 3)

62403016
>>62403005
Are they Ukrainan

62403025
>>62403016
Must be, they are all too thin.

62403081
>>62403005
MMMMMMMMMM FISH!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62403083
>>62403016
They will all wall at 25.

62403090
>>62403005
Stocks are minted out of thin air.

62403099
>>62403005
gold, silver, stocks, FIAT are all printed out of thin air

62403101
>>62403090
and stocks are considered ______

62403106
>>62403099
>just press a button and mint 10,000 oz of gold bro!

62403108
>>62403025
healthy for females
maybe your just a fatty fukker

62403167
>>62403005
Damn, I thought being skinny was 90% of physical attraction for women. These hoes need to eat a pie or something.

62403220
>>62403108
Neighter healthy nor hot nor good for fertility.

62403233
>>62403220
that is borderline chubby to fat

Vicious cicle caused and made on purpose by kikes. Just give up and stop giving your money to kikes it's a casino. Kikes always win

LINK is going back to $1

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-23 22:40 UTC

You guys are honestly such little whiny pussy babies. I am so glad I don't have such a weak ego that I run around fudding LINK all day like some fucking tranny. You guys will legit never be happy, whether LINK is $0 or $1000. Losers.

I wish I never fell for this stupid ass cult.

62403080

item.title: How the hell is the US still outpacing Europe for economic growth after everything that's happened? — 58 replies — 86.5/hr

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62387242
>>62387239
Who cares

62387246
>>62387239
Wendeez nuts hit your chin

62387248
>>62387239
Because GDP has always been a meme.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62390345
>>62387239
>took over the empire
>work harder and more
>have more resources, more land
Truly a mystery.

62390485
>>62387239
Wealth is fake and gay

62390633
>>62387239
Because as much of a shitshow as this country is, everywhere else is somehow even worse

62390680
>>62387239
Because the US is still the place for people who want to make it.

Europe is just fucking stupid for regulations. Whatever you want to do you can't, and if you can it'll be fucking expensive because energy is stupidly high thanks to a bunch of globohomo regulations.

Also most Europeans have a shitty "good enough" mentality, probably because the government cucks them when they make it and has so many gibs it's barely worth trying. A Frenchie wakes up, has a croissant and cafe, goes to the book store at 10 to work until noon, takes an hour for lunch with wine, cheese, stew, works until 4, has more wine and food with friends and calls it a day. They live in a shit flat and rely on the government for basically everything (even their lunch is subsidized) but whatever

62390706
Theyre gay and take too many holidays

62393701
>>62390217
Is there any actual proof that more Americans are moving to Europe than the other way around?

62393779
>> Europe
What do you have, like ten companies of any concern out of your whole crumbling continent?

62394192
>>62390282
What's real then? Your schizo delusions?

62394541
>>62387300
Can we bribe them to do their job?
Would that work????

62394554
The us is pumping more oil and gas than Russia or saudis Arabia

Europe is importing almost all its energy

62394607
>>62387239
Numbers are fake like the Chinese ones.

62394679
>>62387239
>after everything that's happened
what happened? are you retarded?
and how is it hard to outpace europe? europe does nothing

62394688
>>62387239
>How the hell is the US still outpacing Europe for economic growth after everything that's happened?

communist leaders bet on austerity... win stupid prizes

62394698
>>62394688
>communist leaders bet on austerity

Except they didn't, they just spent all their money on stupid shit like windmills and refugee housing programs. The same thing that's happening to canada.

62397412
>>62394679
The tariffs? The strait closing? A bunch of military spending moving away from the US into Europe?

62397491
1) US dollar is still the king of the world especially in cross border settlements.

2)Petro dollar

62401448
>>62387239
The US stock markets is bigger than the next 9 combined!
Go fuck your self yuropoor

62401516
starting a business in europe in general is hell if it isn’t a sole proprietorship. the amount of capital needed to go from 0 to 1 employees is daunting. it’s honestly incredible that there are any European startups at all.

62401538
>>62387239
>economic growth!
Cool, that just means some billionaire is making more money.
You shouldn't downplay where Europe is far ahead, namely in the quality of life, the quality of public services including healthcare and transport, the cleanliness and walkability of cities, food quality, etc etc.
Don't you just love eating chlorinate poisoned chicken because some kike farmer can't be bothered to pay for clean pens?

62401575
>>62387404
>because 2/3 of their GDP dollar value comes from AI or AI related industry
Lmao, that's not how GDP works at all. The top 10 companies for contributing to it are:
1. Amazon
2. Walmart
3. United Health Group
4. Apple
5. Alphabet
6. CVS Health
7. Berkshire Hathaway
8. McKesson
9. Exxon Mobil
10. Cencora
Google, okay fine, we'll call that AI. I'll even give you Amazon just for shits and giggles. That's 2 of our top 10. Europe is poor because they strangle every business in the crib that isn't some 300 year dinosaur.

62401581
>>62387388
>all their researchers are in america
You mean pajeets?

62402324
>>62387239
Tariffs actually make GDP go up if you are a turbo consumer like the US is.
This trick won’t really work for Europe as they are more export driven.

Tariffs are basically a no brainer for any country in the US’s position.
It’s a shame cunts like howard slugshit are trying so hard to sabotage it.

62402357
>>62387239
>How the hell is the US still outpacing Europe for economic growth after everything that's happened?
Because everywhere else is worse than the US, just look at the revelations from people visiting for the world cup
>or the economist article where the """affluence""" of US suburbs rivals EU elite living standards
Almost everything I buy is American or Chinese because no-where else makes anything that I want or need, and the "buy Canadian" propaganda is comical because everything is the same three cottage industries t. leaf

62402388
>>62401538
>namely in the quality of life, the quality of public services including healthcare and transport, the cleanliness and walkability of cities, food quality, etc
…doesn’t that all depend on having the GDP for it in the first place though? I doubt Haiti could achieve Swedish levels of comfort just by copying all of their policies.

62402418
>>62387388
I knew a guy that tried to start a food truck taco stand business in portugal. Took him 13 months to open and his first taco (like the small kind, not mutt size) sold had to be 16€ to even hope to stay afloat.

Theres a critical mass of parasites in EU

62402725
>>62387239
It is illegal and racist to make money in Europe

62402775
>2026
>people still not understanding how the dollar reserve currency system thing works

62402782
>>62387239
Euros are worthless. If we didn't prop them up with the Marshal plan they'd be India tier right now

62403010
>>62402775
Honestly the reserve currency holds the US back if anything

62403133
>>62387239
Eurofags are running faster than burger kikes towards their self destruction.

item.title: If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for. — 141 replies — 64.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

Open thread • Replies: 141 • Updated: 2026-06-23 20:57 UTC

OP

If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

LINK is a $5.7B token on $57.5M of annual fees. That’s ~99x revenue, and it has been flat for years, straight through every “banks are coming” cycle. The promise was always that adoption converts into value. It hasn’t. It’s been “soon” since 2020.

So here’s the question no diehard wants to answer:

What fee number, by what date, would make you sell? If LINK still earns ~$57M in 2028, is the thesis wrong, or is it still “soon”? If you can’t name the number and the date, you’re not investing in Chainlink. You’re believing in it. And belief has no exit.

Early replies (first 3)

62382825
never. Its not about the link token, its just about the hope for the future it gives. That is what keeps me going, even if its a delusion. Thus I will never sell, because as long as I hold the token that fantastical delusion can still be real in my mind

62382842
chatgpt post

62382990
>>62382842
OP is a mentally ill faggot

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62396660
>>62396650
>still emphasizing WORDS
>moar larp
funny how you're hitting every thread lmao
back up that 100x figure on cost and speed, faggot

62397187
>>62396650
>But you should seriously decouple price FUD (totally valid) from tech FUD (makes you sound retarded).

comical. what will it take for you to realize you don't have a piece of ownership of the tech company?

62397245
>>62396341
>>62396420
What about >>62395971
>>62396233
verbose xrp shill and adem in every single thread? these guys claim to not hold the token and yet have been spamming the board for years now, adem since 2021, verbose xrp shill seems like he really showed up around 2023 or so but hard to say for sure. Any thoughts on them? Wouldn't you say its more pathetic to strictly post fud in threads of tokens you don't even own?

62397794
>>62397187
>what will it take for you to realize you don't have a piece of ownership of the tech company?
I realize that.
Chainlink is a cybersecurity company, the security being driven by cryptography and economic incentives. Without the token the entire thing is not better than https. If these peopld truly wrote code for over ten years, only to build useless tech that already exists in other form.
Cryptography is nothing new. Cyprography applied in a decentralized manner, with economic incentives (staking/slashing) is innovative and revolutionary.
If it really is a scam, then it was a very elaborate and convncing one, and I will be willing to admit I was retarded and wrong. If it's not a scam and my thesis is correct then a lot of people on this board will rope. I don't really care if you buy it or not. I don't know or care about you, and you definitely don't have enough money to move the needle on the price.
All I am asking you is to simply take a week and study the protocol before making nonsensical claims.

62397819
>>62397794
>Chainlink
>Decentralized
Lmao. Go run a node and try to get whitelisted to actually being able to get it used and then come back to us.

62397938
>>62397819
>1pbtid
>phoneposting with capitalzations of acronyms
Hi verbose xrp shill I see you are hopping around id's in this thread.

62398220
>>62397819
>Chainlink
>Decentralized
Decentralized does not have to mean the cypherpunk fantasy of "banks aren't needed and everyone is a sovereign individual." It's largely a matter of semantics.
Decentralized simply means that the nodes participating in the network are independent of one another.
If I tell you to watch a football game and report the result back to me in exchange for money, it's difficult to guarantee that you're telling the truth.
If I tell you and another person—whom you don't know—to watch the same game and report the result back to me, my chances of learning the true result increase, because the two of you would have to find each other, coordinate, and risk not getting paid.
Now imagine I tell 20 people to do the same, and on top of that, each person must deposit $1,000 to be eligible to participate and earn money. Nobody has an incentive to lie, because doing so would put their deposit, any future payments for their service, and their reputation as football-game reporters—all of which generate income for them—at risk. That's a decentralized oracle network.
This is the meaning of decentralization and what Chainlink is building - consensus without a chain. Consensus as a service.
I'm done spoon feeding for today. Hope that was clear and helpful to all the perplexed and confused anons here.

62398292
>>62398220
Is McDonald's decentralized because they have 5 employees that I can ask what the price of a big Mac is?

62398491
>>62396382
"token holders don't get any revenue (pause for the clips...) at this point"
>at this point
it's a trick.

62400097
>>62397938
Hi biepum, monitoring the threads I see.
Hows that flippening doing?

62400234
>>62400097
>samefagging fuddie with a fresh new ID complaining about linkies being in link threads

What the fuck is wrong with you.

62400297
>>62400234
>Every post that triggers me is made by the same poster.
Mind breaking heavy bags

62400329
>>62400297
You really have no idea how insanely obvious you are, do you?

62400348
>>62398292
kek
the entire thesis of Chainlink btfo in a single post

62400559
>>62400329
You are deranged lmao, its hilarious how you think you are always talking to 1 person.

62400655
>>62400559
This is a dead board, this thread has been up for almost a week now.
Yet you keep actively seeking out Link threads and then crying about all the "Link shills" you see in them. All the while pretending it's a normal thing to do.

It's not, and you stick out more than ever.

62400679
>dead board
>retarded shill keeps necro bumping his dead shill thread
>hurrrr why does the entire board shit on me?? you're all samefags1!!1!1
stop giving him (You)s and don't forget to sagepost frens

62400691
>>62400679
>shill thread
oof, might wanna check out the OP, samefagboy.

62400897
>>62400655
I just noticed you spamming the thread bieps, just hopped in. Hit you with the reminder of the flippening. You verbally shit yourself again. The regular.

62400962
>>62382813
>white male

no thanks

62401040
>>62400897
>muh flippening
we know you're a xerpie, yes

62401056
>>62400962
Russians arent white

62401136
>>62401056
niggers aren't black

62401340
>still going at it for 5 days
removing IP count was a mistake

62401653
Rome wasn't built in a day.

62401756
Didn't read never selling. Simple as.

62401863
>>62382825
This and the 4.32% roi for doing absolutely nothing

>>62383155
Tbh the only reason literal nobodies as us had a chance to stake was because we held in private wallet a during the 50$ peak, and thus could opt in as I met the onchain criteria, so due to that im happy in the end

62402855
ok, sold the rest, can't stand these anons making excuses anymore. have fun

62402860
>>62402855
Me too!
I'll post my sell order as evidence in a minute

62402872
Ada’s death spiral is glorious. At least they can all sell now and move over to xrp because how fucking stupid they are.

62375140

62375150
>>62375140

62375163

>run by the first trillionaire

>you STILL didn't buy any of their stock

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398213

62398226

62398877
>>62392029

62398970
>>62398090
This animu was a fun watch. Hope you enjoyed it too schadenfreude bro

62398978
>>62375128
>2pbtid

62399080
>>62375150
Bet.

Guess my gains.

62399090
>>62375128
You were saying?

62399160
>>62375140
Well, think of it like this. The right half is all the economic activity that having a jetliner enabled for humanity. Space is vaster still than the air. Space can do what air can do, and more, we can get resources in space. So you have to imagine that the total economic potential of space can be as great if not greater than the economic activity from air travel.
Spacex is the closest of all companies to making it so that the biggest cost to sending something to space is just the fuel to do it. Can they achieve that? If so, then they have a the jetliner of space travel. It would take tens if not hundreds of billions, and at least a decade if not 2 or 3 to replicate what spacex has done, from scratch. Most of their competitors aren't starting from scratch, but they are way behind. If you wanted it done quickly, it would be very expensive, and perhaps not even possible to buy the capability that spacex presents itself capable of delivering.

62399168
>>62375128
The one cow village of OP.

62399196
>>62375128

Anons like OP are like a inverse oracles.

62399203
>>62399196

its priced like a top 10 company but makes no money. Even it does start to make top 10 company profit the price wont go anywhere so yeah

62399214

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 29 replies — 34.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-06-23 22:19 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62396002
Invest only in actual products made by actual companies.

62396879
>>62395695
People have been calling Bitcoin worthless for over a decade. At some point it's worth asking why demand keeps returning every cycle.

62398057
>>62395915
Because I can hold it for the long term while exploring new ways to put it to work.

62398163
>>62398057
Might as well do a bank transfer as it's more private, costs less and the money won't disappear into thin air.

62398819
>>62395655
Because it's not actual Bitcoin, unlike Bitcoin SV (ticker: BSV), which I'm holding.

62399676
>>62398819
BSV is super dead super super dead

62399898
>>62395655
BTC is dead.
Western governments are all over it, they want your Capital Gains via taxes.
Unless you got in early and got out of the country with your gains early you’re fucked. Australia in its recent tax changes has hidden a trap for crypto gains, by stating any earnings made overseas in the last 12 years must be retroactively given to the government to be taxed, if you’re audited and your physical wealth exceeds your earnings, you’re fucked.
Thats outside that statute of limitations for records. It’s a dual purpose instrument- to catch people working in tax havens like Dubai who claimed they were leaving Australia forever then returned 10 years later with unexplained millions, but also to catch crypto investors who were using decentralised exchanges. The ATO has been using bots to crawl through ledgers linking transactions to addresses and piecing together expenditure and entry. If you’re caught it’s not avoidance it’s evasion.

It’s too late now but y’all should’ve moved over to XMR years ago.

62400285
>>62399898
Btc is dead? Wow. Who are you?

62400293
>>62398057
>>62398163
How!!!???

62400366
>>62395655
Worst opportunity by every metric.

62400842
>>62396879
Why do people go to the casino?

62400859
>>62400285
Epstein, Jeffrey Epstein.

62400895
>>62395655
microstrategies fucked it up. he's so maxdebt and overleveraged it's too easy to rip his company apart with the next recession and then take his btc for pennies on the dollar

62400974
>>62400895
also notice the ceo for mstr is white male. we know the results already

62401175
>>62400974
Is he gay? The way he speaks

62401180
>>62400859
Where are you hiding boss? I know you're alive

62401190
>>62400293
Oh no, stop staking your btc

62401192
I hold 3.4btc. is it enough?

62401205
>>62395655
Token not needed.

62401259
>>62395655
My IQ is >85

62402158
>>62395925

62402616
2x pump potential from here AT MOST, while the downside potential (probably $30k bottom) is terrible.

62402679
>>62402158
Same as USA cap gains but canada doesn't give you any incentive to hold long term. TFSA is sick though. I just treat my btc as zero so it keeps me aggressive on the risk curve with my TFSA.

62403029
>>62401192
Buy more. 100 btc

62403094
>>62400293
By native staking through protocols that prioritize self-custody. Simple.

62403095
>>62396002
>Invest only in actual products made by actual companies.
What are you investing?

item.title: KEK tradies are just as fucked by AI as white-collar fags. — 127 replies — 29.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
KEK tradies are just as fucked by AI as white-collar fags.

Open thread • Replies: 127 • Updated: 2026-06-23 18:51 UTC

OP

KEK tradies are just as fucked by AI as white-collar fags. Anthropic found a cheat code to dramatically accelerate robotics using LLMs.

https://www.anthropic.com/research/project-fetch-phase-two

Early replies (first 3)

62392639
So if we're all fucked, what's the endgoal?

62392654
>>62392639
Look what happened to horses

62392668
>>62392639
Cull

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62397384
>>62395201
The world is sick of low iq blue collar workers.

62397402
>>62392639
the singularity
massive central processing warehouses where people will go to merge their consciousness with the computer

62397409
>>62395015
>I have access to self-hosted AGI too
May we see it? Your Claude mythos and blackwell systems?

62397444
>>62397302
They do. The masses are roleplaying being good but their nature is way darker than the elite's. That's the real
>infrared pill

62397478
>>62397444
The masses are low iq and poor.

62397486
>>62392639
pimp

62398293
>>62397478
Which is worse than being evil.

62398312
>>62392533
All the traders I talk to have fucked up knees and backs so not only are they just as worse off but they have fucked up bodies as well

62398353
>>62398293
My daddy always told me being poor is a crime

62398878
>>62392639
Dutch Disease but literally every country, especially the ones who invested the most in education.
Like how a petrostate does the bare minimum of investing in their own population and focuses 99% of their money on improving oil extraction.
AI countries will do the bare minimum of investing in their human populations, with 99% of their money going towards improving the AIs.
You can already kind of see this with the US and China. China gives its citizens jack shit but will happily invest in more robots. The US is sort of the same but more emphasis on thinking machines than robots.
Any country that doesn’t is going to be uncompetitive, though over time the AI dutch disease countries will also become uncompetitive in their own ways as the corruption and poverty rots them within.
In order to prevent mass poverty we’ll need to (in the short term) try to follow the norway model more than the typical petrostate model.
Like how Norway used its oil money to juice its sovereign wealth fund, we’ll need to use AI money on a sovereign wealth fund in order to keep our citizens from completely languishing away.
Long term we need to find ways to synergize human existence with machine existence. So that individuals can also benefit from the endless investment that will be put into the machines.

62398883
Oh look, another lie to prop up the stock price.

62398888
>>62392639
90% into the gulag they are literally unusable
Remaining 9% slave race because they happen to specialize in some field on par or greater to an LLM. They will serve the the remaining 1%

62398909
>>62392759
>>62394987
Why do anons always assume rich fags keep people alive? That they’ll just snap their fingers and everyone dies?
If the rich go for a segregated AI economy, then non AI using people will just continue to live on.
The “poors“ will even build AI robots of their own given enough time.

62398916
>>62393510
sure but how many humans are we talking
certainly not 8 gorillion

62398939
>>62398909
This is the first time in recorded history that a tool threatens with replacing humans as a mean of production. I know some of you dont understand the implications of this, but to pretend this scenario has happened before is retarded.
An AGI powered robot will be able to replace you, me and everyone else. You will not be able to develop your own AGI, because why would the elite give you means for independence? They are sociopaths, they only want you for your money, they are right now threatening you with your replacement telling you they want to replace you straight to your face.

62399166
>>62398939
>You will not be able to develop your own AGI
Kek, wanna bet on that?

62399211
blue collar is 4 losers

62399258
>>62393534
not him but the asian twink in the t-shirt, hes cute. probably has a 2m nw. would like to have sex with him.

62399375
>>62399166
Yes, i bet you don't even have the brain to finetune an open weights model, let alone develop something that still doesn't exist and will (probably) require a new computer hardware paradigm to exist.

62399432
Why can't finance fags understand economics ? It's not that robots can't do wagie shit, they absolutely can they just can't do it economically.

62401372
>>62392639
They'll exterminate us

62401385
>>62392654
>mr hands?

That makes no sense you weirdo pervert.

62401406
>>62393587
They check your prostate via blood tests exclusively now you raging faggot, god you can’t wait for robots to exist so you can shove one into your ass, you people are disgusting

62401454
>>62399432
put an indian brain in a robot's body

62401472
If (when) they can get it working properly and you have robots working 24/7 to build a house then tradies really are over

62402116
>>62398939
>This is the first time in recorded history that a tool threatens with replacing humans as a mean of production.

62402210
>>62402116
what about writing an actual argument instead of posting reaction gifs like a fagjak?

62402214
>>62392654
They moved on from working to fucking white women?

62402229
>>62402214
Shit and curry covered hands typed this

62402259
>>62392639
look up game called "forever winter". That's the endgoal.

item.title: I'm a WHORE for money. — 8 replies — 29.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm a WHORE for money.

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-23 22:53 UTC

OP

I'm a WHORE for money.

Early replies (first 3)

62402151
>>62402137
Sucky sucky 5 dorra?

62402160
>>62402137
Me too. I have crippling sports gambling addition

62402252
What's your "What would it take you to suck a mans cock to completion" amount?
Over the years mine has gone from millions down to about 5k now I would seriously consider it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62402265
>>62402137
I'll send you $1 if you stick a marker up your ass

62402286
>>62402252
I'd pay no more than 600 dollars to suck a cock to completion. Too many cocks to suck out there, and I'm no trillionnaire.

62403015
>>62402137
get into the line

62403104
>>62402137
So you are just a regular whore?

62403212
>>62402252
We should have a dedicated thread on this

item.title: >boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasi… — 118 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasi…

Open thread • Replies: 118 • Updated: 2026-06-23 18:56 UTC

OP

>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago
>been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasing him
>haven't found any jobs, its unreal how bad the market is, have this bad feeling its never going to get better
>PIP ends on Tuesday
I am an engineer. I started off as a tech and worked my ass off getting bachelors full time to escape manual labor. Now im gonna be that goy with a masters degree who took a 500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work. I honestly feel like god did this beacuse its too much bad things happening at once that are culminating into my total demise.

Early replies (first 3)

62388691
>>62388676
>500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work
Trades pay more than ever, especially vs pajeet-saturated office work. Skill issue

62388694
>>62388676
My entire life has been nothing but fighting tooth and nail, working myself 1000x harder than anyone anywhere ever for 0.001% of what anyone else gets for free. I have felt that god is purposely shitting on me pretty much my whole life. It doesn't get better, it only ever gets worse. If anything happens to me that seem to be a good thing, I have 6 months max to enjoy it because that'll be taken from me and then I'll be beaten up and crushed down for added effect. It's a real comedy out there.

So don't worry bro, you have a lot of time ahead of you to suffer far more. If there really is a god, he must be the most perfectly evil of entities.

62388697
The worst part is that in my meeting with my boss today he didnt mention if I will pass the PIP. He said he needed to talk with some people including HR. So 90 percent chance he is going to fire me but just enough chance he wont that I have to spend the weekend waiting instead of processing the pain. And if he doesnt fire me, I will be a totally buckbroken employee who will never care about a rasie and work 60 hours weeks. Like a absurd housewife that has no were else to go and she tries to esapce and gets rapped(applies to ghost jobs) even worse so she comes back to him. Thats me. I used to think my relationship with work was like between a man and a woman, were I am the man that picks and chooses women and is the one who leaves but its all turned around past couple of years. I am nothing but a side pieace female whore to these companies now, they treat me like a contract worker without paying me like one. They got us with not giving raises to match inflation, they got us with return to work mandates and now, they are going to get us by firing us and leaving us with nothing. U will get put on a PIP too, at the worst time possible also. They wont even lay u off anymore, they want to save a little bit of money by denying us unemployment insurance by firing us through a PIP.


Was being in ur 30s a good excperince back in the day? There is no way human civilization would have gotten to were it is if it was always this bad for everyone. Of course, I am a faggot and just suck but the fsct of the matter is that PIPs and no jobs has been a very common trope on the internet lately

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62391843
>>62389431
I'd welcome you to try, sukdeep dikshit. I'll break every bone in your body if you come at me with a saw. Have you ever even operated a saw?

62391855
>>62388729
>>62388736
Retards, mostly 2nd anon.

You get off of backbreaking shit after jman(4-6 years) and specialize. Or sales, or foreman, or estimators, or inspectors, or maint.

Low IQ stay in back breaking

62391907
>>62390573
No, you get unemployment only if you are laid off due to economic constraints or incompetence. Thats why PIPs aren’t that bad. You don’t get unemployment if you are fired for negligence (this can be fought if you have proof they set you up or if they were playing favorites) or if you leave willingly. Unemployment benefits tend to pay up to 2000 dollars a month depending on your salary.
>t. Corporate wagie who dealt with it before

62392212
>>62391843
And who might you be? How about you learn to speak only when spoken to.

62392253
>>62388711
>ah yes did i tell you? all women around me have fancy jobs with big salaries and 100% remote positions and company trips all over the world
the new system is in place permanently and working as intended

62392282
>>62388711
>but i refuse to do it because my gf would earn more than me
Yeah, that’s a very good reason. Never let a girl out earn you.
>t. retired at 25

62392283
>>62392253
> >ah yes did i tell you? all women around me have fancy jobs with big salaries and 100% remote positions and company trips all over the world
Doesn’t bother me. Retired at 25. I know how damaging a 9-5 is and how even waking up at 7am is shitty and terrible for your healthy. Going to bed with an alarm is terrible too. No girl is out earning me. Trust me.

62393076
>>62388802
>I see a nig or a nog here and there
Kek

62393091
>>62388992
Kys

I'm in a very comfy sport right now but most of my life has been a shitshow ripe with suffering. I'll be "grateful" when I'm dead. Until then it's hate, hate, hate and a lot of grit

62393160
>>62388730
May everyone you love die of cancer before your eyes.

62393171
>>62388761
Kys

62393407
>>62388802
This post PROUDLY presented by a jeet.

62393484
>>62391855
Ye bro, let me just causally go from doing interesting work to hammering in nails to build more houses for the jeet and beaner invaders that the boomers are gonna rent out to

62393491
>>62391907
Wait wait wai. I've been tearing my hair out cuz I dont think I will get bennies beacuse i am on a PIP and ur telling me i can still get them? I called the unemployment office and they said 50/50 so idk man. Would be nice

62393565
>>62393091
This is something I have to come to terms with as I get older. My luck isnt the worst b u t the people in my life, especially my biological family has been making.my life hell and I need to cut ties with them once my grandma dies. All the bad things that happened to me in life could have been way easier to deal with without my family making it worse.

62393627
>>62392212
silly esl. you spoke to me and I spoke back. glad you adjusted your energy to match your grip strength, bitch.

62394155
>>62388676
Is that Matteo or Matthew?

62395199
>>62393091
You should be grateful. If not for your suffering, you wouldn't be a successful man. Forgive your mother for making you think life should be like you are a woman.

62395202
>>62393160
I'll still be grateful for what they gave me. Learn to stop acting like an entitled woman. Karen.

62395616
>>62388778
>went to the worst liberal shithole of europe
>why am I struggling??

62395641
>>62388676
>people actually get put on PIPs
bro, how? I have so many workers in my department that don't try for shit at anything. literally nothing happens to them ever. corporate america has some fucked up shit, but generally I've seen the PIP system be less abused than other bullshit

62395717
>>62395641
Not every corporation runs the same
Shocking

62395927
My dream is, if my current gig works out (which will leave me with a lot of cash), is to start a software shop where the work-day is capped at 4 days. I don't care about making it big, I just want to spend my time at work with other people who are happy and live decent enough lives. Since it's an unexploited niche I wager I can gather some pretty decent talent.

Fuck this race to the bottom, I don't wanna be rich I wanna be happy

62395938
>>62395717
>>62395641

It's actually more about your boss. 90% of bosses don't gaf as long as you generally do what they say. There is a 10% of proactive dicks who are absolutely looking to roll heads.

62398374
>>62395927
Just give them wfh and treat them like human beings, engineers dont mind working overtime if its for the right person.


Also ye i had similar dreams to start a plesant working place even at the expense of profits but I mostly want to do it out of spite at this point

62399956
>>62388711
>ah yes did i tell you? all women around me have fancy jobs with big salaries and 100% remote positions and company trips all over the world
jewish daycare

62399988
>>62388694
I hold LINK too, brother

62400021
>>62388676
I was in a similar position like you OP. I disagreed with the staff engineer, he bad mouthed me to manager and I landed on a PIP. I spent 6 months on a PIP, looking for a job (I'm in Germany, they can't really fire me).
Mental was the hardest part. This is a process designed to break you down. What really helped me was to disengage with the work process, say nothing, do the bare minimum, wear my poker face.
After 6 months, I found a new job, negotiated an exit with a severance package.
I am not gonna lie, this was a shitty experience, but I learned a lot from it.

I wish you best of luck OP. Don't let them wear you down.

62401552
>>62388676
AI training is a decent stopgap while you're looking for work. Mercor and Micro1 are best, Handshake's okay, Outlier sucks but doesn't pull shenanigans with your pay (I've heard that Alignerr does).

62402291
>>62400021
Dude im at like 8 months in total i just a took a month long break at the baggining of the year. I would have found a job by now.. I sidnt even disagree with anybody, they are just bullying me and breaking my spirit. Idk why they haven't fired me ye.t today is the last day of my PIP and I have heard nothing. I really just wanna ask my boss if they are gonna can me or not
